channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Stephen A. Smith: Trump may have ulterior motive for Gaetz pick
item.description: Stephen A. Smith says that “under normal circumstances” Matt Gaetz would never be confirmed as the nation's new attorney general.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Trump-district House Democrat loses Alaska seat to political scion
item.description: House Republicans scored a valuable pickup in Alaska's lone seat in the chamber, defeating Democratic Rep. Mary Peltola.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer heading to Mar-a-Lago after being floated for Labor Secretary: sources
item.description: Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer, R-Ore., who is reportedly in consideration to lead the Labor Department, is traveling to Mar-a-Lago at the end of this week, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Florist gets death threats after refusing to serve Trump voters
item.description: The owner posted on social media that the business is now temporarily closed after receiving several death threats. She also made all of her social media accounts private.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Knox, Fox 5

item.title: Trump’s hunt for Treasury secretary down to the wire
item.description: The jockeying for President-elect Trump’s pick to run the all-important Treasury Department is coming down to the wire. Trump’s economic team will be tasked with imposing an aggressive tariff regime meant to shake up global trade while keeping financial markets calm amid the disruption.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: NY bill would expand professionals who can force people to get psychiatric help
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Lawmakers continue to wrestle with how to deal with New York City's mental health crisis.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks
item.description: “A drastic reduction in federal regulations provides sound industrial logic for mass head-count reductions across the federal bureaucracy,” the pair wrote in the op-ed.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: GOP lawmakers reveal a heightened legislative focus against 'irreversible' gender surgery on minors
item.description: Republican lawmakers revealed they will have a heightened focus on legislation related to preventing gender-related medical care for minors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics: Fetterman says he's open to Oz
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, with the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers are concerned about background checks of Trump's Cabinet picks as red flags surface
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As senators prepare to consider President-elect Donald Trump's picks for his Cabinet, they may be doing so without a well-established staple of the confirmation process: an FBI background check.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ZEKE MILLER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Donald Trump's lawyers urge judge to ignore prosecutors and dismiss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As prosecutors push to preserve President-elect Donald Trump’s historic hush money conviction, his lawyers urged a judge Wednesday to ignore them and scrap the case before he takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy lay out plans for 'mass' federal layoffs, rule rollbacks under Trump
item.description: Tech entrepreneurs Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy said Wednesday that their brand-new government efficiency panel will identify “thousands” of regulations for President-elect Donald Trump to eliminate, which they argue will justify “mass head-count reductions” across government.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's cut to Biden's EV tax credit, backed by Musk, may impact auto industry, experts say
item.description: Automotive experts reveal their thoughts on President-elect Trump reportedly considering a slash to the tax credit for electric vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden admin Commerce Dept seeks to exhaust CHIPS Act funding before Biden leaves office
item.description: The Biden administration wants to spend every dollar allocated under the president's Chips and Science Act before President-elect Donald Trump enters the White House, Commerce Secretary Gina Raimondo indicated during an interview this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speaker Johnson announces policy barring trans women from Capitol bathrooms
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) announced Wednesday that transgender women are not permitted to use bathrooms in the Capitol that match their gender identity, taking a stance on the hot-button issue that has overtaken the Capitol in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 things to know about Linda McMahon, Trump's pick for education secretary
item.description: If confirmed by the Senate, McMahon will be put in charge of heading up changes to Title IX and determining how student debt is handled.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jeffries: Republicans don't have a mandate
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Tuesday rejected the idea that voters delivered an Election Day mandate to Donald Trump and the Republicans, arguing that the GOP’s razor-thin majority in the House means that nothing will get done in Washington without Democratic support.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's hush money case should be paused, not dismissed: Prosecutors
item.description: Trump was convicted in May of falsifying business records to cover up a $130,000 payment his former lawyer Michael Cohen made to porn star Stormy Daniels.
item.dc:creator: Luc Cohen / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Defense pick Peter Hegseth paid accuser to protect his career, his lawyer confirms
item.description: The Fox News host reached a settlement to resolve an accusation against him, but a recently released memo brought the issue back into the public eye.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Accused of violating worker rights, SpaceX and Amazon go after labor board
item.description: SpaceX and Amazon are asking the Fifth Circuit Court of Appeals to find the National Labor Relations Board unconstitutional. The federal agency is tasked with enforcing workers' right to organize.
item.dc:creator: Andrea Hsu, MPR News

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC
